evals.prompt: Generate my performance review for the last 12 months. I'm an engineer and I want to see a full breakdown of what I shipped across all my repos and orgs.
evals.expected_output: A complete performance review document saved as performance-review-YYYY.md containing: Executive Summary with 3-5 bullet highlights, Major Projects section with repo-specific contributions, Technical Contributions section, Bug Fixes section, Collaboration section, and Self-Assessment Prompts. Should also produce a performance-review-YYYY.html file with professional styling. The script should check gh auth, run generate_report.py to gather data, spawn subagents per repo for analysis, and synthesize findings.
expectations: Skill checks gh auth status before proceeding
Skill runs generate_report.py to gather GitHub data
Output markdown file named performance-review-YYYY.md exists in working directory
Markdown contains an Executive Summary section
Markdown contains a Major Projects or similar section grouped by repo/theme
Markdown contains a Technical Contributions section
Markdown contains a Self-Assessment Prompts section with open-ended questions
HTML file named performance-review-YYYY.html exists and is non-empty
HTML has a hero header with user name and period

evals.prompt: I have my mid-year review coming up next week. Can you build me a GitHub contributions report for just January through June 2025? I want it to look professional — markdown is fine but I also want the HTML version.
evals.expected_output: The skill identifies the custom time window (Jan 1 – Jun 30, 2025) and passes --since 2025-01-01 --until 2025-06-30 to the script. Produces both markdown and HTML files. The report narrative is framed in business-impact language, not raw commit messages. The HTML report header shows the correct period.
expectations: Skill uses --since 2025-01-01 --until 2025-06-30 (or equivalent 6-month window)
Output files are named with the correct year
Markdown narrative uses business-impact language (not just raw commit messages)
HTML file exists with correct period in hero header
Skill does not ask the user to manually run any gh commands

evals.prompt: write my self-review. i mostly work in python and my main repos are on github under my personal account and two orgs. want to understand what themes emerge from my code this year
evals.expected_output: The skill triggers correctly on 'write my self-review', gathers data from personal account + org repos, and produces a thematic synthesis (not just a flat list of repos). The Major Projects section groups contributions by theme. The Technical Contributions section identifies Python-specific patterns. The output reads like a real performance review a human would write, not a log dump.
expectations: Skill triggers correctly on casual phrasing 'write my self-review'
Report groups contributions by theme rather than just listing repos alphabetically
Report identifies narrative themes across repos (e.g., 'Platform Work', 'Feature Delivery')
Contributions from both personal account and org repos are included
Self-Assessment Prompts section is present and contains relevant questions
Report reads as a narrative document, not a commit log
